app.title: Sidevers
node.muted: Start the embedded protocol node, then dial a peer.
sides.muted: Each side is an independent identity with its own QUIC endpoint (spec §7.6). Click a side to make it active — Connect, Send, and Pair below operate from the active side.
peer.muted: Connect to another node by network address, from the active hosted side.
send.muted: Sent over the active side's open session to its connected peer.
inbox.muted: DMs arriving on this node, newest first.
pair_show.muted: Generate a QR for the active side. Show it on the existing device — a new device scans it (or pastes the URI below) to install this side and become a co-holder. The QR carries a one-time nonce valid for 10 minutes.
pair_show.uri_label: URI (paste this into the other device)
pair_accept.muted: On the new device: paste the URI from the existing device's QR to install its side here.
onboard.welcome.body: Sidevers is a peer-to-peer protocol for talking to the people you've chosen to talk to, without a server in the middle. Each identity you create here is a self-sovereign "side" — your keys, your data, your reach.
onboard.welcome.next: We'll walk through four steps: pick where Sidevers stores your data, create your first side, back up its key, and then you're done.
onboard.dir.body: Sidevers persists your keys, your inbox, and your peer table here. The default is OS-appropriate. Make sure it's somewhere your OS-level disk encryption covers.
onboard.side.body: A "side" is an identity — call it whatever fits the context. Common labels are work, private, public, close. You can add more sides later from the main UI.
onboard.backup.warn: This is the only copy of your private key. If you lose it and never paired another device, your identity is gone — there's no recovery service. Save the file somewhere safe (a USB stick or encrypted vault, not cloud-sync).
onboard.backup.path_placeholder: where to write the seed file
onboard.done.body: Your side address is below. Hand it to someone you'd like to message, or pair your phone with this device from the main UI to host the same identity in two places.
profile.muted: Per-side profile (§7.3). Published to peers who fetch it, and consulted by them to gate which message types they'll send to you (§7.7 capability tokens). Edits below sign + publish under the active side.
contacts.muted: Local-only contacts for the active side (§7.4). The list is never published; each entry's capability set overrides the peer's profile for traffic accepted from them.
